2606.30544.title: Latent Actions from Factorized Transition Effects under Agent Ambiguity
2606.30544.paper_summary: Latent Action Models (LAMs) learn action-like proxies from observation transitions. However, in multi-object or distractor-rich scenes, these visual effects mix agent motion with distractors, camera dynamics, and background changes, making the underlying action source ambiguous without supervision. Structuring this mixture as reusable transition effects provides an intermediate representation from which action-like latents can be more robustly formed. We introduce Observed Transition Factorization (OTF), which decomposes each transition into a sparse set of observed transition primitives. Using these primitives as the transition interface, we propose OTF-LAM, which abstracts motion primitives into action-like latents within the standard inverse-forward dynamics framework, and OTF-LAM-Dino, a decoder-free variant that predicts future states in a frozen DINOv2 representation space. Empirically, OTF primitives transfer zeroshot across controlled carrier and morphology shifts, showing reusability. Furthermore, downstream policy learning results match or outperform baselines under complex transition ambiguity.
2606.30544.paper_summary_zh: 潛在行動模型（LAMs）從觀察轉換中學習類似行動的代理。然而，在多物體或擾動豐富的場景中，這些視覺效應將代理運動與擾動、相機動態和背景變化混合，使得在沒有監督的情況下，潛在的行動來源變得模糊。將這種混合結構化為可重用的轉換效應提供了一種中間表示，從中可以更穩健地形成類似行動的潛在變量。我們引入觀察轉換分解（OTF），它將每個轉換分解為一組稀疏的觀察轉換原語。利用這些原語作為轉換介面，我們提出OTF-LAM，該模型在標準的逆向-前向動態框架內將運動原語抽象為類似行動的潛在變量，並且OTF-LAM-Dino，這是一種無解碼器的變體，能在凍結的DINOv2表示空間中預測未來狀態。實證結果顯示，OTF原語在控制的載體和形態變化中實現了零樣本轉移，顯示出可重用性。此外，下游策略學習結果在複雜的轉換模糊性下與基準相匹配或超越。
2606.30544.author: Heejeong Nam et.al.
2606.30544.authors: Heejeong Nam, Chandradithya S Jonnalagadda, Harshit Aggarwal, Eric Xu, Randall Balestriero
